MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-29-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Syed K. Rizvi of HARRIS BEACH PLLC (212-687-0100) appearing through CourtCall for defendant and moving party Henry Schein.
H.W. Trey Jones of LAW OFFICE OF H.W. TREY JONES (310-498-6254), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiffs David Springer and Dorothy Springer.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Henry Schein' Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED.

Defendant Henry Schein burden shifted to Plaintiffs under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. (See Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 851, 853-855.) Defendant Henry Schein produced Plaintiffs' amended responses to special interrogatories and the dispositions of David and Dorothy Springer. (Rickenbacher Decl. Exs. F, G, and H.) Plaintiffs' responses to special interrogatories failed to provide any evidence that Defendant had the knowledge of the harms of asbestos as alleged. (Rickenbacher Decl. Ex. F, 33:11-27.) Further, Plaintiffs' responses to special interrogatories failed to provide or identify any evidence to support Plaintiffs' conspiracy to defraud claim. (Rickenbacher Decl. Ex. F, 3:19-33:27.) Plaintiffs' failed to file an opposition to this motion. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5915 (Tel: 510-451-1580) csrkim@comcast.net (Tel: 510-589-3651) (503/CML)

MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Apr-29-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Thomas Waskom of HUNTON ANDREWS KURTH LLP (804-788-8403) appearing through CourtCall for defendant and moving party The Scotts Company.
H.W. Trey Jones of LAW OFFICE OF H.W. TREY JONES (310-498-6254), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiffs and opposing parties David Springer and Dorothy Springer.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The Scotts Company's Motion to Strike or in the alternative, request for a section 402 preliminary hearing is DENIED without prejudice and can be renewed at trial. Defendant's do not seek to strike the opinion or testimony from any pleading under California Code of Civil Procedure section 453 or complaint under California Code of Civil Procedure section 452. A hearing under Evidence Code section 402 is to be heard by the trial court outside of the presence of the jury. It is not properly raised at a Motion for Summary Judgment. Accordingly, the motion is procedurally incorrect. Court to issue order. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Kim Richardson, CSR #5915 (Tel: 510-451-1580) csrkim@comcast.net (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-29-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Janice W. Man of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant and moving party Douglass Insulation Company.
H.W. Trey Jones of LAW OFFICE OF H.W. TREY JONES (310-498-6254), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiffs David Springer and Dorothy Springer.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Douglass Insulation's Motion for Summary Judgment is GRANTED.

Defendant Douglass Insulation met its burden of production under Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850, by showing that Plaintiff is unable to show the element of causation for any cause of action. Plaintiff David Springer is the only witness against Defendant Douglass Insulation. (Index of Exhibits Ex. K, Plaintiffs' Witness Statement.) Further, it is undisputed that neither of the Plaintiffs will provide product identification evidence against Defendant Douglass Insulation. (Index of Exhibits Ex. J, Deposition of David Springer p.422:11-17; 450:8-15; Motte Decl. at 1:24 - 2:2.) Plaintiffs' Responses to Douglass' Special Interrogatories, Exhibit C, fail to make any connection between Plaintiffs and Defendant Douglass Insulation that would raise beyond mere speculation. "The evidence creates only "a dwindling stream of probabilities that narrow into conjecture." (McGonnell v. Kaiser Gypsum Co. (2002) 98 Cal. App. 4th 1098, 1105, citing Lineaweaver v. Plant Insulation Co. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 1409, 1421.) Further, Plaintiffs' Responses to Douglass' Special Interrogatories, Exhibit C, state that Plaintiffs cannot reasonably obtain witness evidence for the element of causation because Plaintiffs have no contact information for the other possible witnesses to the alleged exposure. Plaintiff offered no evidence in its opposition to rebut the burden shift and show exposure. Accordingly, Defendant Douglass Insulation's Motion for Summary Judgment is GRANTED. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

Notice Of Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena And Notice Of Taking Videotaped Deposition Of Dr. Brad Black
Off Calendar Mar-25-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Thomas Waskom of HUNTON ANDREWS KURTH LLP (804-788-8403) appearing through CourtCall for defendant and moving party The Scotts Company.
Ana T. Reeg of HAWKINS PARNELL & YOUNG (415-766-3200) appearing through CourtCall for defendant and moving party The Scotts Company.
Solomon Pantuch of HAWKINS PARNELL & YOUNG (415-766-3200) appearing through CourtCall for defendant 84 Lumbar Company.
Robert L. Nelder of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Patterson Company, Inc.
Mirna J. Scheffy of HAWKINS PARNELL & YOUNG (415-766-3225) appearing through CourtCall for defendant The Sherwin-Williams Company.

RULING:
Not argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The Scotts Company's Motion to Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena and Notice of Taking Videotaped Deposition of Dr. Brad Black is GRANTED. Dr. Black is located in Montana, the deposition was to occur in Montana, therefore, the California subpoena is facially invalid under California Code of Civil Procedure section 2020.220. Under California Code of Civil Procedure section 2034.410, the deposition of a non-listed expert shall be governed by California Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.010 et seq. deposition procedures. California Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.280(b) requires a valid deposition subpoena for an out-of-state expert witness. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Joanna Broadwell of VERITEXT LEGAL SOLUTIONS, CSR No. 10959, broadwelljo@aol.com (510-332-0512) (503/CML)

Plaintiffs have failed to submit evidence demonstrating Mr. Carey is an "officer, director, managing agent, or employee" of Defendant Kerr Corporation. (Code Civ. Proc., 2026.010, subd. (b).) Accordingly, the deposition notice served to compel the deposition of Mr. Carey, a non-party and out of state resident, was procedurally improper pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. 2026.010, subd. (c). Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Raquel G. Sharp, CSR # 10619, Raquel@transcriptlegal.com (Tel: 925-384-0003) (503/CML)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Dec-31-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth N. Branham of HAWKINS PARNELL & YOUNG (415-766-3200) appearing through CourtCall for defendant SYD Carpenter, Marine Inc.
Mia Mattis of GOLD LAW FIRM (415-986-1339), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiffs David Springer and Dorothy.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling as follows: Defendant The Sherwin-Williams Company's Motion to Quash Service of Summons or Stay or Dismiss is denied. The Court takes judicial notice of the Complaint pursuant to Evidence Code section 452, subdivision (d) paragraph (1) and the documents filed with the Ohio Secretary of State pursuant to Evidence Code section 452, subdivisions (c) and (h). (Branham Decl., Exs. A-B, E-J.) Notwithstanding the timing of The Sherwin-Williams Company's Motion, The Sherwin-Williams Company failed to cite authority that it has standing to challenge service of process on behalf of Defendant Kool Seal., Inc. The parties do not dispute that The Sherwin-Williams Company and Kool Seal, Inc. are separate and distinct entities. (Jones Decl., 5.) The Sherwin-Williams Company also made a general appearance in this action on November 16, 2020 by filing an Answer. Defendant Kool Seal is a trade name as noted in Exhibit J and description by Ohio's Secretary of State, and not Kool Seal Inc. which is a separate entity. Service to defendant at 101 Prospect Ave, Cleveland, OH is not sufficient. Court to issue order. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and the Court ruled as follows: Plaintiffs' motion is GRANTED pursuant to CCP 36(a) and (d). The trial date is March 8, 2021 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. The tolling of the trial date is April 5, 2021 per the December 10, 2020 Asbestos Case Management Department Order. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is April 22, 2021. The tolling date for the last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is May 21, 2021 per the December 10, 2020 Asbestos Case Management Department Order. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 1, 2021. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. The fact discovery cut-off date is March 19, 2021. The expert discovery cut-off date is April 2, 2021. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than February 23, 2021. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. No later than February 5, 2021, plaintiffs must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. No later than February 5, 2021, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendants' affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on March 24, 2021 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than February 19, 2021. Responses to the demand are due no later than March 5, 2021. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by March 26, 2021 at 12:00p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, April 1, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Court to issue order. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Raquel G. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-24-2020 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant University of Southern California's Demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiffs' claims for fraud (third cause of action) and conspiracy to defraud (fourth cause of action) must be pled with specificity. (see e.g., Roberts v. Ball, Hunt, Hart, Brown & Baerwitz (1976) 57 Cal. App. 3d 104, 109 ["Every element of the cause of action for fraud must be alleged in the proper manner and the facts constituting the fraud must be alleged with sufficient specificity to allow defendant to understand fully the nature of the charge made"]; Favila v. Katten Muchin Rosenman LLP (2010) 188 Cal. App. 4th 189, 211 ["because fraud was the object of the conspiracy alleged by the Estate, the claim must be pleaded with specificity"].) As for premises liability (fifth cause of action), Plaintiffs fail to identify the premises at issue. Plaintiffs have ten days to amend the complaint from the date of this Order. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-24-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant University of Southern California Motion to Strike is GRANTED. Plaintiffs fail to state sufficient facts to support a claim for punitive damages. (Cyrus v. Haveson (1976) 65 Cal. App. 3d 306, 316-17 ["To support punitive damages, the complaint asserting one of those causes of action must allege ultimate facts of the defendant's oppression, fraud, or malice"].) Page 12, line 20 through page 13, line 4 of the complaint are stricken in their entirety. Page 13, item 2 under Prayer for Damages is also stricken. Plaintiffs have ten days to amend the complaint from the date of this Order. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
